title: OZ Apartment
shortDescription:
The project is shaped by a sense of quiet restraint. The spaces feel unhurried - warm oak tones and soft plaster surfaces run continuously through every room, creating a seamless flow rather than a collection of separate areas.
The layout plays with volume: pitched ceilings and curved architectural elements give the interiors a sculptural quality, letting light move across surfaces in unexpected ways. A tall arched window anchors the living-dining zone, drawing the landscape inward and blurring the boundary between inside and out. Material-wise, the palette is deliberately narrow - matte wood grain, veined marble, ribbed ceramic tile, and linen-toned plaster. Nothing competes. The textures are tactile but subdued, meant to be felt more than noticed. Dark accents in metal and upholstery ground the warmth without breaking the calm. The overall atmosphere is one of deliberate simplicity - a space that breathes, where natural light and honest materials do most of the work.
